course_description:
This course introduces how to develop and maintain effective and inclusive learning environments in higher education programs. It draws on contemporary theories of higher education pedagogy and uses an evidence-informed reflective approach to improve practice. Participants attend common workshops and in other weeks, engage in learning activities specific to their chosen specialisation. A detailed schedule for each specialisation stream will be available on Blackboard.

Participants must have current teaching or course design responsibilities. These are defined more broadly than course coordination and include any role supporting learning or curriculum development in formal, informal, or professional settings. Examples include lecturing, lab demonstrating, PhD supervision, tutoring, designing workshops or online modules, creating extracurricular programs, or developing learning resources such as videos or assessment guides. They also include mentoring, supervising placements, developing digital tools, and contributing to student engagement or professional learning. In summary, any activity that facilitates learning or enhances educational experiences.

In addition to course credit, successful completion of Curriculum Design at UQ (EDHE6002) and Teaching in Practice (EDHE6003) within three years can also lead to conferral of HEA Fellowship, subject to external moderation.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
companions: We recommend completing the following courses at the same time:
restrictions: Restricted to Graduate Certificate in University Teaching Practice students. This course has a maximum quota of 50. Applicants who meet the prerequisites will be given first preference, followed by ranking based on appointment classification and type, with priority given to continuing staff.

course_contacts.name: Associate Professor Christine Slade
course_contacts.notes: Please email me in the first instance briefly outlining your key enquiry, so appropriate arrangements can be made.

course_aims: This course introduces strategies for developing and maintaining effective and inclusive learning environments in higher education programs. It aims to assist participants address contemporary challenges impacting university educators in Australia and internationally. The course draws on contemporary theories higher education pedagogy, taking an evidence informed reflective approach to improving practice. The course introduces a variety of digital media, universal design and accessibility principles, and strategies to evaluate the effectiveness of strategies adopted in practice. Participants will attend the common seminars and engage in the learning activities particular to their specialisation choice in other weeks.

learning_outcomes.description: Critically review current practice and use an evidence-informed approach to plan a strategically aligned enhancement initiative
Implement and evaluate the effectiveness of an enhancement initiative.
Collaborate with members of a community of practice in teaching and learning at UQ
Critically reflect on personal learning across course and impact on professional practice and personal development.

assessment_summary.title: Presentation and Dialogue
assessment_summary.due_date: Negotiated selection of date & time in weeks commencing 30/03/26 or 04/05/26.

assessment_details.title: Enhancement Proposal
assessment_details.task_description: The best way to enhance your teaching is to start with a solid plan. This task will help you to review your current practice, identify a strategic change you can make this semester to enhance your approaches and environments and to support and guide learners. You will identify the supports you need to put in place to succeed. You will complete a template: • drawing together information you already have including your teaching philosophy and context, • identifying people you need to work with and tasks you need to complete, • strategically planning your change and the support you need to get there. The template will be provided through Blackboard (Learn.UQ) and we will work through this template in Workshop 1 identifying support available in this course and beyond to implement your enhancement plan. Feedback on this task will be discussed in Workshop 2. AI permitted use: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly acknowledge any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to acknowledge generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submitted through Blackboard (Learn.UQ)
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A resubmission opportunity may be provided where submissions are marked as a Fail, any resubmission opportunities and conditions will be at the discretion of the Course Coordinator.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A resubmission opportunity may be provided where submissions are marked as a Fail, any resubmission opportunities and conditions will be at the discretion of the Course Coordinator.

assessment_details.title: Presentation and Dialogue
assessment_details.due_date: Negotiated selection of date & time in weeks commencing 30/03/26 or 04/05/26.
assessment_details.task_description: Pitch your change and get feedback through this presentation and dialogue This task challenges you to refine your plan for how you will implement your change to 4-minute presentation. You will then engage in a 4-minute dialogue, responding to questions on rationale, methodology, and potential impact to securely demonstrate your work. You may be completing this assessment before you implement your change, alongside your change or soon after your change. A PowerPoint template will be provided to support your presentation on Blackboard (Learn.UQ). The presentations and dialogues will be held on campus with two assessors and small group of your peers. This oral presentation and dialogue is to be completed in-person. This task will be recorded to support assessment activities by the teaching team and review as required by HEA accreditation and UQ Assessment Procedures. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted in your presentation and dialogue. You may use AI to support your preparation. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Peer Practice Observation Report
assessment_details.task_description: You will be required to submit a completed template signed off by a peer who has seen you engaging with your students around the change you have implemented through this course. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.

assessment_details.title: Case Study and Reflection
assessment_details.task_description: In this culminating task, you’ll develop a case of your practice and reflect on your professional learning journey through the Teaching in Practice program. Drawing on your Enhancement Proposal, Change Presentation, and Peer Observation, you will demonstrate how your practice has evolved, grounded in the HEA Professional Standards Framework (PSF) and ready to use in your Annual Performance & Development (APD). This reflective task not only showcases your growth as an educator at UQ but also provides authenticated evidence of your effectiveness. AI permitted use: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly acknowledge any use of AI or MT in each instance. learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 15 (16 Feb - 07 Jun)
learning_activities.topic: Self Directed Learning Throughout this course you will have time dedicated to engage with literature, resources and your teaching. This time will support you planning, reflecting on and implementing your changed teaching practice. You will be responsible for your independent learning to achieve the outcomes of this course. Learning outcomes: L01, L04

Workshop 1: Course Introduction This workshop will introduce the course, providing an overview of assessment tasks, activities, and concepts. In this workshop you will meet the course staff and make significant progress towards completing assessment 1. This workshop will be delivered in face-to-face mode only and is compulsory for continuing the course. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 4 To Week 14 (09 Mar - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Specialisation Modules Throughout this course you will select at least one specialisation module. Specialisation modules involve synchronous and independent activities. In these modules, you will work with experts in the specialisation area and work with tools or resources to help identify opportunities for enhancement, guide reflection on your practice, and implement changes. Learning outcomes: L01, L03

Workshop 2: Feedback, Presentation and Dialogue, and your Peer Learning Community This workshop will explore the feedback from Assignment 1, prepare you for Assessment 2 and introduce you to your Peer Learning Community (PLC). Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
Presentation and Dialogue (Assessment 2) - Option 1 Pitch your change and get feedback through this presentation and dialogue. Learning outcomes: L01, L04

Peer Learning Community #1 Supportive peer to peer social learning groups that meet regularly to support and challenge to enable exploration of ideas and gather feedback on change project and enhancements in a safe, supportive environment. There are two timetabled peer learning community (PLC) activities and you are welcome to connect with your PLC throughout the semester. Learning outcomes: L01, L03
Presentation and Dialogue (Assessment 2) - Option 2 Pitch your change and get feedback through this presentation and dialogue. Learning outcomes: L01, L04

Workshop 3: Writing your Case Study and Reflection This workshop will support your writing about your teaching in preparation for assessment 4, annual planning and development and other contexts. Through this workshop we will be reflecting on your experiences through this course aligned to the Professional Standards Framework. We will also be developing our case studies and preparing you for future enhancements to your teaching practice. Learning outcomes: L02, L04

Peer Learning Community #2 Supportive peer to peer social learning groups that meet regularly to support and challenge to enable exploration of ideas and gather feedback on change project and enhancements in a safe, supportive environment. There are two timetabled peer learning community (PLC) activities and you are welcome to connect with your PLC through out the semester. Learning outcomes: L01, L03

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
